definition: Quantitative tightening raises the term premium on sovereign bonds, but the frequently cited 30-60bp magnitude is poorly identified. With N=2 QT episodes — neither clean, as fiscal issuance roughly doubled during QT2 — confidence intervals include zero on the low end and the full observed ~100bp on the high end. A fundamental identification problem that may never be resolvable.
evidence: Vayanos-Vila preferred habitat theoretical foundation is sound
N=2 QT episodes, neither clean for identification
Fiscal issuance roughly doubled during QT2, making attribution impossible
Total observed term premium move ~100bp but share attributable to QT unidentifiable
Pair_2 debate established this as a fundamental identification problem
open_questions: Can QT-specific (mean-reverting) vs fiscal supply (persistent) term premium components be empirically separated?
Does the June 2024 taper ($60B to $25B/month Treasury cap) provide a usable natural experiment?
Do QT and fiscal supply effects compound additively or partially substitute?
